intro:
There is no information available on a plant called 'Aria alpina'. However, I can provide you with information on the plant 'Arnica alpina', which is also known as Alpine Arnica. 

Arnica alpina is a flowering plant in the daisy family, native to the European Alps. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and swelling, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of Arnica alpina include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Arnica alpina is commonly used in herbal preparations such as topical creams, ointments, and tinctures, often used to treat muscle and joint pain, bruises, and sprains.
